description: A designed public-service-announcement style promotional graphic in portrait format for ICE's Victims of Immigration Crime Engagement (VOICE) office. The right two-thirds is a soft-focus stock photograph of two pairs of hands clasped together in a comforting/supportive gesture (one person consoling another seated person in a domestic interior with curtains), conveying empathy and reassurance. The left third is a semi-transparent blue overlay panel bearing white sans-serif text: 'If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to' followed by a very large bold 'HELP'. A diagonal orange accent shape cuts across the lower-right corner. A white footer bar spans the bottom containing the phone number '1-855-48-VOICE' (blue) and 'ICE.GOV/VOICE' (orange), a vertical divider, then the VOICE logo (a blue stylized 'V'/checkmark mark) with 'VOICE' and the subtitle 'VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT'. The corporate blue/orange palette and reassuring imagery present ICE as a compassionate victim-services provider. Propaganda technique: emotional appeal/empathy framing that reinforces the narrative that immigration is linked to victimization while positioning the agency as a helper and protector. Target audience: the general public and self-identified victims, plus broader messaging that immigrants commit crimes against Americans.
notable_text: If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to HELP; 1-855-48-VOICE; ICE.GOV/VOICE; VOICE - VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT
